answer: On September 15, 2026, the Florida purchase escrow disbursement reconciliation was overstated because pipeline settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 joined settlement.escrow_disbursement_fact to party.party_identity_bridge using normalized_tax_id instead of closing_party_role_id. Since normalized_tax_id can match multiple buyer/seller roles within the same closing, it duplicated escrow disbursements in finance.disbursement_recon_daily. The fix was to switch the join key to closing_party_role_id, quarantine normalized_tax_id joins in reconciliation workloads, and backfill Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title.

facts.affected_segment: Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.remediation: Switch the transform to closing_party_role_id, quarantine normalized_tax_id joins in reconciliation workloads, and backfill Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.root_cause: settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 joined party_identity_bridge by normalized_tax_id instead of closing_party_role_id, causing duplicate escrow disbursements because normalized_tax_id matches multiple buyer and seller roles within the same closing
